perturbation.original_text: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, then summarized in a writing so marked by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Recipient Party with fifteen (15) days. Confidential Information shall also include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, then summarized in a writing so marked by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Recipient Party within fifteen (15) days. Confidential Information shall NOT include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall also include' to 'shall NOT include', there's a direct contradiction in the definition of Confidential Information. This creates a contradiction because section 1 A (ii) establishes specific requirements for the recipient party to treat the terms of the agreement as confidential, but this change would exclude these terms from being 'Confidential Information.' This could create an issue if the recipient party fails to comply with section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care. B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance. C. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than to one of the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives. D. Not copy or otherwise duplicate such Confidential Information, or knowingly allow anyone else to copy or otherwise duplicate any such Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text in section 1A explicitly includes 'the terms of this agreement' as Confidential Information. The changed text explicitly excludes them. Section 2 then outlines the recipient party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information. By excluding the terms of the agreement from being Confidential Information, the changed text creates a contradiction because the obligations in section 2 no longer automatically apply to the terms of the agreement, as they would have under the original text. This means the recipient party is no longer explicitly obligated to protect the confidentiality of the agreement itself under Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose, including the Evaluation referenced above, without the Disclosing Party of such information in advance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above' to 'including the Evaluation referenced above' creates a contradiction. Section 2 B originally permitted the use of Confidential Information for evaluation, but now it prohibits it unless authorized. This contradicts the core purpose stated in the WHEREAS clause, leading to uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, SYMMETREX and Other Party (jointly the "parties") wish to disclose to each other certain information solely for the purpose of evaluating a proposed business arrangement between the parties (the "Evaluation"); and WHEREAS, in consideration of and as an inducement for and condition of such disclosure, each party hereby requires the other party to agree to the terms and conditions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the use of confidential information for the evaluation, aligning with the stated purpose in the WHEREAS clause (Evaluation). The changed text prohibits using confidential information for any purpose, including the evaluation, unless explicitly authorized. This directly contradicts the WHEREAS clause, which establishes the evaluation as the primary reason for information disclosure, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Provided such notification is given, the Recipient Party is hereby authorized to comply with such judicial, administrative or governmental order.
perturbation.changed_text: Even if such notification is given, the Recipient Party is NOT authorized to comply with such judicial, administrative or governmental order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is hereby authorized' to 'is NOT authorized' creates a contradiction. The original text allows compliance with a government order if the Disclosing Party is notified. The modified text prohibits compliance, contradicting the ability to adhere to legal mandates and creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in the event that any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is required to be disclosed by the Recipient Party by judicial, administrative or other govern mental order. Such notification shall be made reasonably promptly so that the Disclosing Party, at its expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. Even if such notification is given, the Recipient Party is NOT authorized to comply with such judicial, administrative or governmental order. The Recipient Party shall provide the Disclosing Party reasonable cooperation (at the expense of the Disclosing Party) in connection with any protective order or other remedy sought by the Disclosing Party in connection with such order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient Party is 'NOT authorized' to comply with a governmental order, 'Even if such notification is given'. The contradicted text includes the same sentence. This direct and unambiguous language creates a clear and impactful contradiction, as the original text allowed compliance with notification, but the modification prohibits it, leading to a contradiction regarding the Recipient Party's authorization.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party shall return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party may choose to return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall return' to 'may choose to return' creates a contradiction. Section 5 originally obligated the Recipient Party to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request. Now, it's optional, contradicting the confidential nature of the information and weakening the Disclosing Party's control over it. This undermines the entire purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care. B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance. C. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than to one of the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives. D. Not copy or otherwise duplicate such Confidential Information, or knowingly allow anyone else to copy or otherwise duplicate any such Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Recipient Party 'shall return' confidential information, creating an obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may choose to return', making the return optional. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, particularly section 2.A which states the Recipient Party 'shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence'. Making the return of confidential information optional fundamentally undermines the obligation to hold it in strict confidence, thus a strong contradiction exists within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that the other party has no legal obligation pursuant to this agreement to disclose information to it hereunder but is doing so as an accommodation to the Recipient Party in reliance upon the terms of this agreement and that all information received by the Recipient Party hereunder cannot be relied upon as a representation or warranty of the Disclosing Party and, accordingly, no Disclosing Party shall be liable for any reliance upon any information disclosed by it hereunder, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that the other party has a legal obligation pursuant to this agreement to disclose information to it hereunder but is doing so as an accommodation to the Recipient Party in reliance upon the terms of this agreement and that all information received by the Recipient Party hereunder cannot be relied upon as a representation or warranty of the Disclosing Party and, accordingly, no Disclosing Party shall be liable for any reliance upon any information disclosed by it hereunder, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'no legal obligation' to 'a legal obligation' creates a contradiction in Section 6. This directly contradicts the nature of a *non*-disclosure agreement, which typically doesn't *require* disclosure. This is further complicated since WHEREAS clause makes the agreement conditional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, SYMMETREX and Other Party (jointly the "parties") wish to disclose to each other certain information solely for the purpose of evaluating a proposed business arrangement between the parties (the "Evaluation"); and WHEREAS, in consideration of and as an inducement for and condition of such disclosure, each party hereby requires the other party to agree to the terms and conditions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that there is 'no legal obligation' to disclose information, while the changed text asserts there 'a legal obligation' to disclose information. This directly contradicts the WHEREAS clause, which states the parties 'wish to disclose' information. The change introduces a mandatory aspect to disclosure that is inconsistent with the conditional nature of the agreement as outlined in the WHEREAS clause. This is a strong contradiction as it alters the fundamental purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: If either Party breaches its promises made in this Agreement, the non-breaching Party will have available to it all remedies available under law.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Party breaches its promises made in this Agreement, the non-breaching Party will have no recourse and waives any right to remedies available under law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all remedies available under law' to 'no recourse and waives any right to remedies available under law' creates a direct contradiction. The original section 10 provides the non-breaching party with legal remedies, while the modified version eliminates them entirely. This contradicts the enforceability and purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOW, THEREFORE, for adequate consideration acknowledged to be received by each party, each party, intending to be legally bound, hereby agrees as follows: 1. For all purposes of this agreement, the following definitions shall apply: A. "Confidential Information" of a party shall mean any trade secrets, know-how, inventions, products, designs, methods, techniques, systems, processes, software programs, works of authorship, business plans, customer lists, projects, plans, pricing, proposals and any other information which a party discloses to the Recipient Party that: (i) if disclosed in writing is clearly marked as confidential or carries a similar legend; or (ii) if disclosed verbally or in tangible form is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, then summarized in a writing so marked by the Disclosing Party and delivered to the Recipient Party with fifteen (15) days. Confidential Information shall also include the terms of this agreement, the fact that the information is being made available to the parties hereunder and the fact that the parties are contemplating a possible business arrangement. B. "Disclosing Party" shall mean, with respect to any information disclosed by one party to the other, the party which, directly or through one of its agents, discloses such information. C. "Recipient Party" shall mean, with respect to any information disclosed by one party to the other, the party which receives such information. 2. Each Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information received by it hereunder shall: A. Hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and protect it in accordance with a standard of care which shall be no less than the care it uses to protect its own information of like importance and in no event less than reasonable care. B. Not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except as is necessary for the Evaluation referenced above or as otherwise authorized in writing by the Disclosing Party of such information in advance. C. Not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than to one of the Recipient Party's directors, officers, employees, representatives, consultants or agents (collectively called "Representatives") who need to know such information for the purposes of the Evaluation, provided that each Representative shall be informed, directed and obligated by the Recipient Party to treat such information in accordance with the obligations of this agreement and the Recipient Party shall be liable for breach of any such obligation by any of its Representatives. D. Not copy or otherwise duplicate such Confidential Information, or knowingly allow anyone else to copy or otherwise duplicate any such Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior written approval. 3. Each Recipient Party shall notify the Disclosing Party in the event that any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party is required to be disclosed by the Recipient Party by judicial, administrative or other govern mental order. Such notification shall be made reasonably promptly so that the Disclosing Party, at its expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. Provided such notification is given, the Recipient Party is hereby authorized to comply with such judicial, administrative or governmental order. The Recipient Party shall provide the Disclosing Party reasonable cooperation (at the expense of the Disclosing Party) in connection with any protective order or other remedy sought by the Disclosing Party in connection with such order. 4. The obligations imposed on a Recipient Party with respect to Confidential Information shall not apply to Confidential Information disclosed to it hereunder which (a) the Recipient Party can demonstrate is, at the time of disclosure, already known to the Recipient Party, (b) is or becomes generally known to the public or is otherwise deemed to be in the public domain through no wrongful act of the Recipient Party, (c) is received from a third party who has the legal right to so furnish such Confidential Information, or (d) the Recipient Party can demonstrate is independently developed by or for the Recipient Party without use of Confidential Information. 5. Upon the written request of the Disclosing Party with respect to any Confidential Information disclosed by it hereunder, the Recipient Party shall return to the Disclosing Party any or all of such information and all copies, contracts or other reproductions in whole or in part thereof or, with the written permission of such Disclosing Party, effect the destruction of all of same and certify such destruction in writing to the Disclosing Party. 6. Each party acknowledges that the other party has no legal obligation pursuant to this agreement to disclose information to it hereunder but is doing so as an accommodation to the Recipient Party in reliance upon the terms of this agreement and that all information received by the Recipient Party hereunder cannot be relied upon as a representation or warranty of the Disclosing Party and, accordingly, no Disclosing Party shall be liable for any reliance upon any information disclosed by it hereunder, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party. 7. Nothing contained in this agreement shall be construed as granting to either party any right, title or interest in any information disclosed hereunder to it, except for the limited right to use and disclosure as expressly permitted hereunder. 8. Each Recipient Party shall on any copies it may make of any information received hereunder maintain any and all copyright, patent or trademark notices which may appear on such information when so received. 9. Each party represents that the disclosure by it of information hereunder does not violate the rights of any other entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the non-breaching party would have all remedies available under law, which implies that the agreement is enforceable and provides legal recourse for breaches. The changed text removes any recourse and waives the right to remedies. This contradicts the general principle outlined at the beginning of the contract in the contradicted text, where both parties intend to be legally bound. By eliminating legal remedies, the modified section undermines the very foundation of the agreement and renders the intention to be legally bound meaningless, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.
